author: Spokane Way
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM target unless record README says otherwise
notes: Exact current-session rerun used the standalone record-local script on 8xH100 in the uv-managed baseline environment.
This host reproduced the script far faster than the source README run (52.65 ms/step vs 71.47 ms/step), increasing completed steps from 8394 to 11397 before the same 600 s cap.

The faster execution produced a much better post-quant val_bpb than claimed, improving by -0.00919078. This is a major discrepancy and should be treated as a host/runtime difference rather than compared naively against the historical claim.
submission_blurb: SP-1024 9x512 KV4 run at TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096 with aggressively tuned Muon optimizer: momentum 0.99, lower LR (0.020/0.020/0.030), 3/4 batch (393K tokens), warmdown 3000 steps, and extended momentum warmup (1500 steps from 0.92). Combines long-context training with training optimization to beat the naive baseline by 0.023 BPB.
claimed_method_summary: SP-1024 9x512 KV4 run at TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096 with aggressively tuned Muon optimizer: momentum 0.99, lower LR (0.020/0.020/0.030), 3/4 batch (393K tokens), warmdown 3000 steps, and extended momentum warmup (1500 steps from 0.92). Combines long-context training with training optimization to beat the naive baseline by 0.023 BPB.
